title: Repair phase3 deployment to the PR commit by isolating the failing deploy-class step and completing the server update
objective: The PR is already created. The only current blocker is phase3_update_server_to_pr_commit. General executor result persistence is not the blocker, because the smoke task returned a terminal result. Treat this as a deploy-class task repair. Isolate which deploy sub-step hangs or fails, then complete the server update to the exact PR commit and prove it.

mandatory_references.why: Current top-level blocked state and next-step context.
Original deploy task contents and acceptance.
Proof that the executor can still return terminal results for cheap tasks.

required_work.description: Start by explicitly classifying the blocker as deploy-class task failure or hang, not as general executor result-persistence failure, unless fresh evidence disproves the existing smoke-task proof.

Break the original deploy task into stepwise remote probes with terminal-result checkpoints after each probe. At minimum isolate: repo path/probe, git fetch/checkout target commit, docker compose status probe, and API restart/health probe.
Once the failing sub-step is isolated or repaired, complete the deployment so the server actually runs release commit 0c2cd3e8e607deb4fb3b886c20161b44a867d353.
Prove the final deployed state with exact git HEAD, service/container status, and API health evidence.
If and only if deployment is proven complete, state that the main top-level PR/deploy/validate task can resume at phase4_post_deploy_debug_validation. Do not silently keep going in this task.

stepwise_probe_requirements.goal: Confirm actual repo root used on server and prove it can be accessed.
acceptance: Return ls or equivalent proof that repo exists

stepwise_probe_requirements.goal: Confirm target branch/commit is fetchable and checkout-able.
acceptance: Return resulting git rev-parse HEAD after checkout attempt

stepwise_probe_requirements.goal: Confirm docker compose context and API service visibility before restart.
acceptance: Return docker compose ps output or equivalent for api
Return compose file path actually used

stepwise_probe_requirements.goal: Restart only the API service and verify health.

do_not: Do not reopen PR scope questions.
Do not re-run the whole top-level PR/deploy/validate task from scratch.
Do not keep doing blind reset/retry loops without stepwise evidence.
Do not classify the blocker as general executor result-chain failure unless a fresh smoke task now fails too.
Do not proceed to phase4_post_deploy_debug_validation until the server update is proven.
working_principles: Because smoke-task terminal result already succeeded, treat this as a deploy-class repair unless new evidence disproves that.
Split the deployment into smaller executor tasks so the first failing sub-step is visible.
Use fresh evidence at every sub-step.
Stop at the first real blocker if deployment still cannot be completed.

deliverables.description: Evidence bundle for deploy-step probes, repair attempts, and final deployment proof.
Detailed main-controller log for this deployment repair task.

acceptance: The first failing deploy sub-step is identified with evidence, or the deployment is completed.
If completed, API service status is healthy and proven.
If not completed, the first real blocker is precisely classified as deploy-step-specific, infrastructure-specific, or environment-specific.
